title: $TIMEOUT
text: $TIMEOUT Generate a message after an inactive period. $TIMEOUT { = } Minutes Defaults Assignment file: Any application assignment file Section: [SETTINGS] Default value: None Description $TIMEOUT generates a message after Minutes minutes have elapsed during which the user has not been active in the application; a new message is generated after each period of Minutes in which the user has not been active. You can set a maximum value of 10080 minutes, which equals one week time. The message contains the string 'Timeout'. This interrupt can be handled in a receiveMessage trigger, allowing the application to be closed cleanly. For example: trigger receiveMessage if ($result = "Timeout") ; perform close-up activities ... apexit endif end; receiveMessage trigger Copy This setting is available only on Microsoft Windows. Example: $TIMEOUT [SETTINGS] $TIMEOUT = 20 Copy Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
